#!/bin/sh
# commit-msg hook: reject commit messages that would close a GitHub issue by
# closing-keyword adjacency — including across a line wrap (mg-2627).
#
# Install: git config core.hooksPath hooks
#
# This is the EARLY half of the check. It fails while the message is still
# cheap to fix, but it protects only people who installed it, and --no-verify
# walks straight past it. The guarantee lives in the refinery, which runs the
# same detector on every branch it merges — see
# internal/refinery/closingref_gate.go. If you skip this hook, the refinery
# still catches it; you just find out after pushing.

set -e

msg_file="$1"

# Prefer a built binary, then one on PATH, then build from source. Never skip:
# a check that silently no-ops when the binary is missing reports "clean" for
# the exact commits nobody verified.
repo_root=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
if [ -x "$repo_root/bin/pogo" ]; then
    "$repo_root/bin/pogo" check-commit-body "$msg_file"
elif command -v pogo >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    pogo check-commit-body "$msg_file"
else
    (cd "$repo_root" && go run ./cmd/pogo check-commit-body "$msg_file")
fi

echo "commit-msg: ok"
